D5 Design L4 Analyze Reference

Subpixel Colour Fringing

ClearType sharpens text by lighting individual R, G, B subpixels — and pays for it with faint coloured edges

An LCD pixel is three vertical stripes — red, green, blue. Subpixel anti-aliasing (ClearType) treats each stripe as its own sample, tripling horizontal resolution for sharper stems. But where an edge lands mid-pixel, the three stripes get different coverage, so the boundary tints: red on one side, blue on the other.

Nudge the black stem across the subpixel grid and switch between plain greyscale anti-aliasing and subpixel. Greyscale stays neutral but softer; subpixel is crisper but its edges shimmer red and blue as it moves.
